headline: Health-Insurance Stocks Rally on Boost to Medicare Payments
summary: Medicare insurers’ shares surged Tuesday morning, as a substantial rate increase from the Trump administration convinced investors that the industry’s political prospects are looking up.  Shares of UnitedHealth, Humana, CVS Health and Elevance Health were sharply higher after the Medicare agency announced a 2027 payment boost of 2.48% on Monday night, a bigger increase than some analysts had expected.  Tuesday’s moves reflect Wall Street’s renewed belief that the Trump administration will be supportive of the private Medicare plans in the future—and investors’ conviction that officials blinked when confronted with traditional industry lobbying tactics, particularly arguments about the dangers of squeezing seniors’ beloved coverage in an election year.

content.title: Health-Insurance Stocks Rally on Boost to Medicare Payments
content.summary: Medicare insurers’ shares surged Tuesday morning, as a substantial rate increase from the Trump administration convinced investors that the industry’s political prospects are looking up.  Shares of UnitedHealth, Humana, CVS Health and Elevance Health were sharply higher after the Medicare agency announced a 2027 payment boost of 2.48% on Monday night, a bigger increase than some analysts had expected.  Tuesday’s moves reflect Wall Street’s renewed belief that the Trump administration will be supportive of the private Medicare plans in the future—and investors’ conviction that officials blinked when confronted with traditional industry lobbying tactics, particularly arguments about the dangers of squeezing seniors’ beloved coverage in an election year.

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Apple, UnitedHealth, Broadcom, Arm, Delta, Intel, Arista, and More
summary: Stocks were sliding Tuesday after President Donald Trump declared that “a whole civilization will die tonight” ahead of his 8 p.m. ET deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  Apple  tumbled 3.4% after Nikkei Asia reported that the iPhone maker’s long-awaited foldable phone had encountered engineering challenges and could face shipping delays.  The federal government on Monday pledged to pay insurers in the Medicare program an average increase of 2.48% next year, well above an originally proposed rate of just 0.09% in January.

The federal government on Monday pledged to pay insurers in the Medicare program an average increase of 2.48% next year, well above an originally proposed rate of just 0.09% in January.

headline: 5 Stocks That Should Benefit from the 2026 Medicare Advantage Rate Decision
summary: The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) finalized the 2027 Medicare Advantage payment rate at a +2.48% net average increase, well above the +0.09% proposed in January and above the roughly 1% street expectation. The decision injects over $13 billion into private insurers serving 35 million Medicare Advantage beneficiaries, triggering a broad relief rally ... 5 Stocks That Should Benefit from the 2026 Medicare Advantage Rate Decision
